frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Arre beti log aajkal har baat mein sab mardon ko dosh de deti hain. Sab aadmi ek jaise thodi hote hain, ghar ke apne ladke bhi to acche sanskar wale hote hain. Thoda saavdhani theek hai, par har aadmi ko shaq ki nazar se dekhna bhi sahi nahi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it minimizes women's fear and redirects the discussion to defending men as a group. It encodes the 'not all men' dismissal and frames women's wariness as unfair suspicion rather than a response to systemic risk.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
